message:
test(codex): scaffold subprocess integration harness for hook protocol [git_safety_guard-ovw4.3.1]

Adds tests/codex_hook_protocol.rs — the P2.1 scaffold the rest of
Phase 2 (P2.2 through P2.14) builds on. Mirrors the existing
tests/agent_hook_output.rs pattern but covers BOTH protocols, since
the Phase 2.9 parity matrix and the fail-open scenarios in Phase 2.10
need to spawn dcg with and without `turn_id`.

What it provides:

- HookOutcome { stdout, stderr, exit_code, stdin_sent, home_dir }
  with `stdout_str()`, `stderr_contains(s)`, `is_codex_block_shape()`
  (exit==2 + empty stdout + non-empty stderr), `is_claude_block_shape()`
  (exit==0 + stdout contains hookSpecificOutput JSON), and
  `is_allow_shape()` (exit==0 + empty stdout). A Display/Debug impl
  prints a postmortem block (stdin pretty-printed, stdout as UTF-8 +
  hex dump for ≤256 bytes, stderr as UTF-8, exit code, hermetic HOME
  path) so any failed `assert!` carries enough context to diagnose
  without rerunning under --nocapture.

- Payload builders matching real-world clients:
    `build_codex_payload(cmd)`  — full Codex 0.125.0+ shape with
        session_id, turn_id, transcript_path, cwd, hook_event_name,
        model, permission_mode, tool_name, tool_input.command,
        tool_use_id (Codex's parser is strict about missing fields).
    `build_claude_payload(cmd)` — Claude Code shape per
        code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks (no turn_id — that's the
        disambiguator).

- Hermetic subprocess runner (`run_hook_raw`):
    - env_clear() then a minimal known-good env (PATH from the
      parent so dcg can find python3 if needed, NO_COLOR=1)
    - per-test isolated HOME via tempfile::tempdir() — isolates
      DCG_HISTORY_PATH, ~/.config/dcg/pending, ~/.config/dcg/
      allowlist.toml, and any other HOME-relative state. Without
      this, parallel nextest runs would race on the pending-
      exception store.
    - per-test isolated TMPDIR inside the test HOME.
    - DCG_TEST_KEEP_TEMPDIRS=1 leaks the TempDir for postmortem
      filesystem inspection and prints the path to stderr.

- Ergonomic public wrappers: `run_codex_hook(cmd)`,
  `run_codex_hook_with_env(cmd, extra_env, _remove_env)`, and the
  matching Claude pair. (`_remove_env` is reserved — env_clear()
  already gives a blank slate, so leaf tests just opt-in via
  extra_env. Kept in the signature so Phase 2.x leaves don't have
  to refactor when removal becomes meaningful.)

- Five smoke tests that exercise every scaffold helper before
  downstream leaves depend on it:
    1. Codex + safe command → allow shape
    2. Claude + safe command → allow shape
    3. Codex + destructive (`git reset --hard HEAD~1`) → block shape
       (exit 2 + stderr deny)
    4. Claude + same destructive → block shape (exit 0 + JSON)
    5. Hermetic-HOME assertion: a Codex deny lands its pending
       exception (if dcg writes one) inside the test HOME, never in
       the real HOME.

Compiles against existing dev-deps (`tempfile = "3.14"`, `serde_json
= "1.0"` already declared in Cargo.toml). Workspace-relative dcg
binary discovery uses `current_exe()` so cargo test / cargo nextest
both work without extra setup.

Closes the scaffolding requirement of git_safety_guard-ovw4.3.1.
P2.2 through P2.14 will land as separate commits.
